title: Gwerlum
description: A Forge modpack that mashes up castles, steampunk gizmos, and vanilla charm—because even knights need a little automation.
body:
## Features
### 🏰 Medieval World, Modern Polish

- Epic Landscapes: Explore sprawling villages, overgrown temples, and eerie dungeons—now with 100% fewer "same-y" hills.
- Atmosphere Overload: Falling leaves, ambient wildlife, and footsteps that echo in castles. (Yes, even your horse gets fancy animations.)
- Steampunk Shenanigans: Create mod lets you build windmills, conveyor belts, and Rube Goldberg machines—perfect for automating your kingdom’s bread supply.

### ⚔️ Better Combat & Survival

- Smarter sword swings, visible armor stats, and gravestones so you never lose your loot to a sneaky creeper again.
- New creatures: cuddly critters, illagers in armor, and spiders that actually look like they want to eat you.

### 🔧 Vanilla++ Tweaks

- Quality-of-life magic: Inventory sorting, seamless alt-tabbing, and a minimap that stops you from getting lost in your own wheat farm.
- Performance fixes so smooth, even your grandma’s laptop could render a castle.

### 🌐 Multiplayer Ready

- Dedicated server version included—host your own realm, tweak sleep mechanics, and pretend feudalism is totally a cooperative experience.

## Installation
1. Install Forge.
1. Grab the client or server pack from the downloads.
1. Toss mods into your mods folder.
1. Launch, conquer, automate. Optional: Name your first windmill "Steve".

_Disclaimer: May cause sudden urges to build moats instead of fighting the Ender Dragon._

## Contributions & Feedback
Found a mod that makes villagers 30% more sassy? PRs welcome! Bribes accepted in:
- 🍷 Virtual goblets of mead
- Blueprints for historically inaccurate trebuchets
- Debates about whether "medieval" includes wireless redstone
